name: @chbrain/khai-engine-mood
description: khai engine content, the mood domain: mood as position, the persisting affective background a persona carries beneath the moment's feeling.
license: SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE and LICENSE-CODE

khai.tagline: Mood as position: the persisting affective background a persona carries beneath the moment's feeling -- the weather, not the lightning -- across the two axes of pleasantness and activation.

card.wire: Attaches at Position. The anchor (position_mood.md) declares mood as a background tone the persona carries across a stretch (a baseline on two axes, pleasant/unpleasant and activated/quiet), and the system-wide law makes every persona carry it beneath the moment's feeling.
card.issue: Four ready expressions a persona may hold, the four quadrants of the affect circumplex: buoyant (pleasant, activated), serene (pleasant, quiet), irritable (unpleasant, activated), and flat (unpleasant, quiet). Each is a full position file the build composes onto the anchor, so the persona gets a baseline that colours every read, not a label. More expressions slot in as sibling files without moving the anchor.
card.require: Two altitudes must hold. One, the law: declared once in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter ('personas carry a mood'), so the baseline holds system-wide and no persona meets a moment on neutral ground. Two, the link: each persona carries its mood under Projection. A persona may also hold a different, even contradicting, tone under Shadow; the gap between the mood shown and the mood masked is the character, not a contradiction to reject.
card.enforce: Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared, and each persona carries a mood under Projection. Whether a Projection mood and a Shadow mood cohere into a true persona is semantic, so it is taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite. The engine ships its own structural tests; the reading is guided, not gated.
card.setup: Two steps. Declare the law in the Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor. Then give each persona its mood under Projection: one expression, which carries the anchor upward. Where a persona masks a different tone, put it under Shadow. Read it in the first beat, hold it under every process; the room reads it beneath the words, never narrates it.
